source_paper_title: Zero-Sum Mean-Field Dynkin Games: Characterization and Convergence
context:
Section 4 (Propagation of chaos), Remark 4.1, page 27 (PDF page numbering may differ; appears immediately after Corollary 4.1).

The question of convergence of S-saddle points of the particle system to those of the limit process is more elaborate and will be addressed in a forthcoming paper.
title: Convergence of \,S\,-saddle points from interacting Dynkin games to the mean-field Dynkin game limit
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{0\le t\le T},\mathbb P)\) support a Brownian motion and an independent Poisson random measure, and fix \(p\ge 2\). Consider a zero-sum mean-field Dynkin game with value process \(V=(V_t)_{0\le t\le T}\) characterized by a mean-field doubly reflected BSDE, with payoff obstacles given by measurable maps \(h_1,h_2:[0,T]\times\Omega\times\mathbb R\times\mathcal P_p(\mathbb R)\to\mathbb R\). Assume the game admits (for each \(S\in\mathcal T_0\)) an \(S\)-saddle point \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\) defined by the first hitting times
- \(\tau_S^\star := \inf\{t\ge S: V_t = h_1(t,V_t,\mathbb P_{V_t})\}\),
- \(\sigma_S^\star := \inf\{t\ge S: V_t = h_2(t,V_t,\mathbb P_{V_t})\}\),
where \(\mathcal T_0\) denotes the set of \((\mathcal F_t)\)-stopping times taking values in \([0,T]\), and \(\mathbb P_{V_t}\) is the law of \(V_t\).

For each \(n\in\mathbb N\), consider the corresponding system of \(n\) weakly interacting zero-sum Dynkin games with value processes \(V^{i,n}=(V^{i,n}_t)_{0\le t\le T}\), \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\), depending on the empirical measure \(L_n[V^n_t]:=\frac1n\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{V^{j,n}_t}\). Assume the interacting game admits \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) defined by
- \(\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n} := \inf\{t\ge S: V^{i,n}_t = h_1(t,V^{i,n}_t,L_n[V^n_t])\}\),
- \(\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n} := \inf\{t\ge S: V^{i,n}_t = h_2(t,V^{i,n}_t,L_n[V^n_t])\}\).
Assume the propagation of chaos / convergence of values holds, e.g. \(V^{i,n}\to V^i\) where \(V^i\) are i.i.d. copies of \(V\), in a norm such as \(\|V^{i,n}-V^i\|_{\mathcal S^p}\to 0\), and \(L_n[V^n_t]\to \mathbb P_{V_t}\) in Wasserstein distance.
problem_definition_latex: Prove a convergence (as \(n\to\infty\)) of the interacting-game \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) to the mean-field \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\), in a mathematically precise sense (e.g. convergence in probability of stopping times, or convergence in law of the pairs \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) to \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\), possibly for fixed \(i\) and/or jointly for finitely many indices).
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{0\le t\le T},\mathbb P)\) support a Brownian motion and an independent Poisson random measure, and fix \(p\ge 2\). Consider a zero-sum mean-field Dynkin game with value process \(V=(V_t)_{0\le t\le T}\) characterized by a mean-field doubly reflected BSDE, with payoff obstacles given by measurable maps \(h_1,h_2:[0,T]\times\Omega\times\mathbb R\times\mathcal P_p(\mathbb R)\to\mathbb R\). Assume the game admits (for each \(S\in\mathcal T_0\)) an \(S\)-saddle point \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\) defined by the first hitting times
- \(\tau_S^\star := \inf\{t\ge S: V_t = h_1(t,V_t,\mathbb P_{V_t})\}\),
- \(\sigma_S^\star := \inf\{t\ge S: V_t = h_2(t,V_t,\mathbb P_{V_t})\}\),
where \(\mathcal T_0\) denotes the set of \((\mathcal F_t)\)-stopping times taking values in \([0,T]\), and \(\mathbb P_{V_t}\) is the law of \(V_t\).

For each \(n\in\mathbb N\), consider the corresponding system of \(n\) weakly interacting zero-sum Dynkin games with value processes \(V^{i,n}=(V^{i,n}_t)_{0\le t\le T}\), \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\), depending on the empirical measure \(L_n[V^n_t]:=\frac1n\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{V^{j,n}_t}\). Assume the interacting game admits \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) defined by
- \(\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n} := \inf\{t\ge S: V^{i,n}_t = h_1(t,V^{i,n}_t,L_n[V^n_t])\}\),
- \(\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n} := \inf\{t\ge S: V^{i,n}_t = h_2(t,V^{i,n}_t,L_n[V^n_t])\}\).
Assume the propagation of chaos / convergence of values holds, e.g. \(V^{i,n}\to V^i\) where \(V^i\) are i.i.d. copies of \(V\), in a norm such as \(\|V^{i,n}-V^i\|_{\mathcal S^p}\to 0\), and \(L_n[V^n_t]\to \mathbb P_{V_t}\) in Wasserstein distance.

Prove a convergence (as \(n\to\infty\)) of the interacting-game \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) to the mean-field \(S\)-saddle points \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\), in a mathematically precise sense (e.g. convergence in probability of stopping times, or convergence in law of the pairs \((\tau_{S}^{\star,i,n},\sigma_{S}^{\star,i,n})\) to \((\tau_S^\star,\sigma_S^\star)\), possibly for fixed \(i\) and/or jointly for finitely many indices).
